dant (seventh house) of the child's birth chart.
Fertilization takes nine days. If fecundation does not take
place within fourteen days after menstruation, it ceases to
function. Hence when we trace backward from the birth chart
of a native, this period does not always correspond with the
time of coitus.
The duration of pregnancy is said by our ancients to be of
273 days. Modem medical science places it at 278 days. A
child born 181 days after gestation can survive. The uper limit
cannot be beyond 321 days.
When the duration of pregnancy is 8,9 or ten lunations,
the Moon's place at the time of germination falls on the ascen
dant at birth, provided the Moon at birth is below the horizon
-i.e. behind the Sun. If the Moon at birth is above the horizon,
the Moon's place at germination will roincide with the descen-

84 Rectification of Birth Time
dant of the birth chart. There are other possibilities also. Thus
